characters.name: Caporegime
characters.description: CaporegimePhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGendermBehind the scenesAppears inGoodfellasCaporegime also known as "Skipper" is an important figure inCosa Nostra. A Caporegime is allowed to start his ownFaction"Crew" and usually makes the most money and new operations for the family.

characters.name: Frankie Carbone
characters.description: Frankie CarbonePhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGendermBehind the scenesAppears inGoodfellasFrankie Carbone (1941 - 1984, aged 43) was a member of theLucchese crime family. Carbone was involved in the JFK Airport heist; his purchase of a mink coat for his wife was treated as a potential giveaway that he and his gang were involved in the crime. He was later killed byJimmy Burketo prevent him from ratting, and to take his share of the money. His corpse was hung up in a meat truck; it took authorities two days to thaw his body out for the autopsy.

characters.name: Henry Hill
characters.description: Henry HillPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHeight6’1” (Real Henry Hill) 5’9”Weight204 lbsBehind the scenesAppears inGoodfellas Born on June 11, 1943 in Brooklyn, New York, Henry Hill grew up in an area of Brooklyn that was dominated by the Lucchese crime family. Fascinated by gangsters, he began dreaming of being in the Mafia at age 12. The son of an Irish father and Sicilian mother, Hill could never be a "made" Mafia member because he wasn't a full-blooded Italian, but his charm and cunning made him welcome inside the Lucchese crime family. Hill soon became a close associate and friend of Paul Vario, one of the more respected capos in the family. In his teenage years, Hill would do errands for Vario and his crew, and eventually moved to more serious crime. His time in the Mafia would span three decades, beginning in 1955.

characters.name: Jimmy Burke
characters.description: Jimmy BurkePhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGendermHeight5’10 ½“Weight193 lbsBehind the scenesAppears inGoodfellas Jimmy first gained respect by refusing to flip over on a fellow criminal in 1949 over a check-cashing scheme. The police beat him savagely as a result, but Jimmy never caved in. Jimmy spent 5 years in prison afterward, but his reputation preceded him and he gained further status by performing murders for mob figures who were also in prison. Jimmy became quite fond of committing murder over the years. Just before getting married in 1962, Jimmy was told of an ex-boyfriend of her fiancee who gave her problems. Jimmy reportedly chopped the man into pieces and left him "tossed all over the inside of his car." One time Jimmy also murdered the son of an elderly woman that had refused to repay her and gave her the $5000 himself.

characters.name: Paul Cicero
characters.description: Paul CiceroAliasesPaulieRelativesTuddy Cicero(brother)WifeSons, nephewsAffiliationLucchese crime familyCicero CrewOccupationMobsterCrime bossBusinessmanBiographical informationMarital statusMarriedDate of birth1925Place of birthEast New York, Brooklyn, New York City, New YorkDate of death1988Place of deathFort Worth Federal Prison, Fort Worth, TexasPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHeight6'4"Weight320 lbsEye colorBrownBehind the scenesPortrayed byPaul Sorvino Paul"Paulie"Cicerois a main character inGoodfellas. He is acapoin theLucchese crime familyand a hugely feared and respected mobster in East New York. He is portrayed byPaul Sorvinoand based onPaul Vario. Paul Cicero was born to an Italian (Sicilian)-American family in East New York, Brooklyn in 1925, the older brother ofVito, nicknamed "Tuddy" and his other siblings. From a young age, he joined theLucchese crime familyand made numerous connections in and around his neighborhood, including workers atIdlewild Airportin Queens, through which he was able to steal cargo and other merchandise coming through the airfield. Sometime before the 1950s, Paulie's cunning and criminal expertise was recognized by his bosses and he was made acapoin the family in charge ofhis own crew, and he mainly ran it from thePitkin Avenue cabstand, which was officially under Tuddy's name. Over the years, he became a cautious, quiet and calculating mob boss, and he was said to "move slow", but only because he "didn't have to move for anybody".

Over the years, he became a cautious, quiet and calculating mob boss, and he was said to "move slow", but only because he "didn't have to move for anybody".

characters.name: Paul Vario
characters.description: Paul VarioPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGendermBehind the scenesAppears inGoodfellas Paul, or "Paulie" as his friends called him, was known for his size. He was 6’0” and weighed close to 300 pounds the majority of his life. His appearance resembled that of a sumo wrestler, with jowls hanging below his chin, likening his appearance to that of a ferocious bulldog. He moved slowly through life because he felt that he didn't have to move quick for anyone and figured things would bend to his will, one way or another. His speaking often consisted of short grunt-like responses. Paulie owned a flower shop at Fulton Avenue in New York as well as a number of other businesses, including an auto junkyard, and bar-restaurant, where he and his cronies liked to hang out. At the bar, men asked Paulie for help and many deals were made, even severe punishments. Paulie lived with his wife Phyllis and three sons: Paul Jr., Peter, and Lenny.

characters.name: Soldato
characters.description: SoldatoPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGendermBehind the scenesAppears inGoodfellasSoldato also known as "Wise guy" is the first position in theCosa Nostraladder. A Soldato starts working in aCaporegime's crew and usually does the jobs needing to be taken care off.

characters.name: Tommy DeVito
characters.description: Tommy DeVitoPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHeight5’5”Weight160 lbsBehind the scenesAppears inGoodfellas Thomas"Tommy"DeVitois the tritagonist ofGoodfellas. He is an unhinged, maniacal murderous mobster who works underPaul CiceroandJimmy Conway. He is alsoHenry's best friend and partner. He is portrayed byJoe Pesciand based on the real-lifeLucchese crime familymobsterThomas DeSimone Tommy is first introduced as a young boy in 1955 working as a fence forJimmy Conway. It is then that he is introduced toHenry Hill, who Jimmy also employs. Jimmy explains to them that they'll be working together from now on, to which they both agree. When Henry gets busted by the police for selling cigarettes, Tommy runs off and tellsPaulie's Crewabout it; the charges against him are later dropped.
